videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA released the Nemotron 3 series consisting of Nano, Super, and Ultra models. The models combine hybrid Mamba transformer architecture with Latent Mixture of Experts and MTP to differentiate from other open models.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 is here!
videos.summary: AI Search reviews Claude Fable 5 (also referred to as Claude Mythos 5), demonstrating its capabilities across tasks including image recognition, ray tracing, Earth simulation, 3D reconstruction, music composition, chemistry instruction, and medical image classification. The review compares Fable 5 against GPT 5.5 and covers model specs and performance benchmarks.

videos.title: Google's Agents CLI: The CLI + Skills Combination to Ship AI Agents EASILY
videos.summary: Google's Agents CLI is an open-source tool that enables developers to build and deploy AI agents to production quickly. It provides agent identity, sandboxed code execution, and audit trails for production-grade AI agents. The CLI works with various AI coding assistants including Claude.

videos.title: I One-Shotted An Entire App With Claude Fable 5 (Full Breakdown)
videos.summary: Jordan Urbs demonstrates building a video processing app using Claude Fable 5 from only screenshots, completing it in one hour with minimal intervention. He cautions that while the capability is impressive, Fable 5's elevated token burn rate and temporary availability through June 22 create financial trade-offs compared to Claude Opus, recommending builders evaluate whether the speed gain justifies the cost.

videos.title: Build a calendar app in AI Studio in 6 minutes
videos.summary: Google Cloud demonstrates building a no-code calendar application using Google AI Studio and Gemini. The tutorial shows how to extract travel itineraries and convert them into calendar invitations using advanced reasoning capabilities.

videos.title: Don't use Fable 5 in Claude… do this instead
videos.summary: David Ondrej advises against using Fable 5 in Claude, recommending an alternative method instead. The video title signals a negative stance on Fable 5 integration with Claude.

videos.title: Stop Making Models Bigger, Make Them Behave — Kobie Crawdord, Snorkel
videos.summary: Kobie Crawford argues that smaller models trained with reinforcement learning to use tools correctly and self-correct outperform much larger models that hallucinate. A 4B Snorkel-finetuned model trained with under $500 solved a SQL task that Qwen 3 235B failed, and single-table training transferred to multi-table tasks with significant benchmark gains.

videos.title: The Problem Solvers | Michael Truell at Cursor
videos.summary: Cursor is an AI-native coding platform that helps engineers and less technical builders ship quality code through agents. Founded two years prior, the company grew from 15 people to 700 employees and now serves over 60% of Fortune 500 companies.

videos.title: PAI: The Life OS for AI-Powered Development
videos.summary: PAI (Personal AI Infrastructure) is a Life OS built on Claude Code that provides persistent memory, custom skills, structured workflows, and a seven-phase Algorithm to retain project context across AI sessions. It addresses the problem of AI agents forgetting architecture, coding standards, and prior decisions between sessions.

videos.title: Sovereign Escape Velocity: Ownership w Open Models — Gus Martins, & Ian Ballantyne, Google DeepMind
videos.summary: Gemma 4's 31B model achieves competitive quality on the LM Arena leaderboard while running on a single GPU, compared to competitors requiring four to five GPUs. Variants include 2B and 4B parameter versions optimized for mobile and edge deployment, and the license shifted from custom Gemma to Apache 2.0 to enable sovereign adoption across institutions in emerging markets.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Design – Full AI Design Workflow 2026
videos.summary: Claude Design is an AI design tool that replaces manual design work and Figma for generating branded landing pages and design systems. The tutorial demonstrates a 4-step workflow including design system building, asset generation, editing, and template deployment with token management guidance.

videos.title: Self Driving Products: Product Signals to Pull Requests — Joshua Snyder, PostHog
videos.summary: PostHog is building an autonomous agent pipeline that converts product signals like errors, rage clicks, and customer messages into pull requests. The system groups related signals using LLM-generated query embeddings, researches codebases, and opens PRs automatically.

videos.title: What are AI agents?
videos.summary: Google released the Agent Development Kit (ADK), a framework for building autonomous multi-agent systems. The video demonstrates building a self-correcting blog-writing agent using ADK, covering the ReAct Framework, three agent patterns (sequential, reactive, planning), and practical implementation with validation and error-correction loops.

videos.title: Can you social engineer an AI? Plus: AI worms and the nonhuman identity problem
videos.summary: IBM Security Intelligence podcast episode examining social engineering vulnerabilities in AI systems, self-replicating AI worms, and nonhuman identity security risks. The episode discusses whether AI agents are more susceptible to manipulation than humans and explores emerging threats including prompt injection attacks and autonomous agent propagation.

videos.title: Anthropic Just Dropped Fable 5 And It’s Terrifying
videos.summary: Anthropic released Claude Fable 5, a Mythos-class frontier AI model with strong capabilities in software engineering, knowledge work, vision, analytics, scientific research, and cybersecurity. The release includes built-in safety systems that restrict access to high-risk capabilities in areas like cybersecurity, biology, and chemistry.

videos.title: Apple’s New Siri AI Is Now Gemini
videos.summary: Apple unveiled a rebuilt Siri AI at WWDC 2026 with personal context, screen awareness, and Visual Intelligence, powered by Google Gemini. Despite the upgrade, investor reaction was negative with Apple shares falling, and EU regulatory delays complicate the rollout. The piece questions whether Apple is advancing AI capabilities or merely repackaging competitor technology.

videos.title: Claude Mythos is FINALLY here (Fable 5)
videos.summary: Anthropic released Claude Fable 5, its first Mythos class model, which achieves top performance on multiple benchmarks including SWE Bench Pro and Frontier Code. The model supports vision capabilities, extended context for long-running tasks, and demonstrates performance on code generation and full-stack application development.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 beats Pokémon FireRed only using vision
videos.summary: Claude Fable 5 successfully completed Pokémon FireRed using only raw game screenshots without maps or navigation aids. This represents an advance over earlier Claude models which required complex helper infrastructure to accomplish similar game-playing tasks.

videos.title: AI and the Job Market in 2026
videos.summary: AWS Developers discusses how AI is reshaping tech hiring and job hunting in 2026. The video covers networking strategies, personal branding, and argues that junior developers remain valuable despite AI automation, using historical job market evolution as precedent.

videos.title: Anthropic's Brand New AI Just Started the Gold Rush (Fable 5)
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Anthropic's new Claude Fable 5 model and its applications in regulated industries. The video covers new use cases for the model and introduces "Know Your Agent" methodology for effective context engineering in long-running AI applications.

videos.title: Claude just dropped UltraCode... its Insane
videos.summary: Anthropic released UltraCode, a Claude feature that spins up up to 10 parallel agents managed by code instead of a master LLM. The tutorial covers parallel agent patterns, judge panels, adversarial verification, and deterministic fan-out for building agentic operating systems with optimal model selection via OpenRouter.

videos.title: Build an app from your phone in AI Studio
videos.summary: A tutorial on using Google AI Studio to build a mobile-browser-based image-generation app with voice commands and the Nano Banana API. The demo shows Gemini generating code in real time to create a plushie app without local software installation.

videos.title: Behind the Scenes - Introduction to Artificial Intelligence with Brian Yu - Chapter 1 - Playing
videos.summary: CS50 is Harvard University's introduction to computer science and programming, taught by David J. Malan. The course covers AI fundamentals and is available through multiple platforms including edX, Harvard Extension School, and OpenCourseWare, with an active global community across numerous social channels.

videos.title: GPU Cloud Deployment Without Leaving Your IDE — Audry Hsu, RunPod
videos.summary: Flash is a decorator-based framework that enables single-command GPU cloud deployment from an IDE with hot reload capabilities. It replaces multi-step Docker and registry workflows with @flash.endpoint annotation on async Python functions, supporting model chaining and serverless autoscaling on RunPod infrastructure.

videos.title: Sarah Paine - Why Putin and Xi can't escape geography
videos.summary: Military historian Sarah Paine outlines a two-thousand-year geopolitical framework dividing continental empires that pursue security through territorial conquest from maritime powers that pursue prosperity through trade. The lecture examines how geography has shaped global power dynamics and examines the behavior of contemporary powers like China and Russia through this lens.

videos.title: Claude Mythos 5 + Fable 5 Are Here And The Numbers Are INSANE
videos.summary: Anthropic has released the Claude Mythos 5 model family and Fable 5, a variant with built-in guardrails. Both models are now generally available with impressive performance metrics. Fable 5 is being offered at no extra cost through June 22 on Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise plans, with pricing expected to shift to usage credits afterward.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 plays Factorio
videos.summary: Claude Fable 5 autonomously plays Factorio, a factory-building game, demonstrating the model's ability to strategize and execute complex multi-step tasks involving planning and resource management without human intervention.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 simulates the solar system and predicts a solar eclipse
videos.summary: Claude Fable 5 built a simulation of the solar system by deriving planets' orbital motion from physics first principles and used it to predict solar eclipses. This demonstrates the model's ability to perform scientific reasoning and simulation.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 sets a fluid simulation to Beethoven
videos.summary: Claude Fable 5 generated a fluid simulation coded from scratch and produced an EDM remix of Beethoven using code, synchronizing the motion to the music beat. The demonstration showcases Claude's ability to generate both visual simulations and audio without prior exposure to music.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 designs a 3D-printable model in a Claude-built CAD editor
videos.summary: Claude Fable 5 is demonstrated designing a 3D-printable model using a browser-based CAD editor. The CAD editor and its integrated AI copilot were both built by Claude Fable 5.

videos.title: RAG is dead, right?? — Kuba Rogut, Turbopuffer
videos.summary: Kuba Rogut argues that RAG is not dead but poorly defined. RAG encompasses vector search, full text search, glob, regex, and iterative agent-based filtering, not just vector lookup. Real usage data contradicts the discourse: Google search volume for RAG spiked in mid-2025 while social media declared it dead.

videos.title: GPU-accelerated virtual drug screening with cuML and Agent Platform
videos.summary: A Google Cloud livestream presentation showing how GPUs accelerate virtual drug screening using cuML and Agent Platform. The demo achieves 20x-45x training speedups on molecular prediction tasks by replacing CPU-bound pandas and scikit-learn workflows with GPU-accelerated cuDF and cuML equivalents.

videos.title: From Transcription to Live Music: Gemini's Audio Stack — Thor Schaeff, Google DeepMind
videos.summary: Gemini 3 Flash Preview provides audio understanding including speaker identification, timestamps, emotion detection, language detection with translation, and summarization in a single API call. Gemini 3.1 Flash Live is a real-time multimodal sound-to-sound model with integrated reasoning capabilities for interactive audio and speech applications.

videos.title: Stop Prompting Claude. Use Karpathy's Method Instead.
videos.summary: Austin Marchese presents Andrej Karpathy's method for using Claude more effectively, structured as three layers designed to accelerate development velocity. The approach contrasts with typical prompting patterns and emphasizes a systematic methodology that unlocks Claude's potential.

videos.title: How To Design a Complete Brand Identity With AI (Best AI Logo Maker)
videos.summary: Design.com is an AI platform for generating complete brand identities including logos, brand kits, business cards, and social media templates. Users input plain-English descriptions and edit outputs through conversational interface. The tool auto-applies brand styling across 500,000+ templates without manual re-coloring.

videos.title: Introducing Gemini 3.5 Live Translate
videos.summary: Google DeepMind introduced Gemini 3.5 Live Translate, a speech-to-speech translation model supporting 70+ languages with real-time multilingual translation and automatic language switching. The tool is available to developers via the Gemini API and enables live dubbing of video content with natural-sounding output.

videos.title: Kimi Code is good at video reasoning with Kimi K2.6 🎬
videos.summary: Kimi Code is a video reasoning tool built on Kimi K2.6 that accepts reference videos and generates .cube LUT (color lookup table) files based on visual queries about colors, shots, or visual style. Users can drag in video references and request specific visual attributes to generate ready-to-use color grading outputs.

videos.title: How I'd Learn AI From Scratch in 2026 (skip the useless 80%)
videos.summary: Jeff Su presents a three-level roadmap for learning AI from scratch: picking one model to go deep with, mastering context over memorization, and building AI systems using tools. The guidance emphasizes skipping inefficient techniques and focuses on practical workflows using modern LLMs.

videos.title: Finally… A NotebookLM Alternative With an API
videos.summary: Open Notebook is a self-hosted, open-source alternative to Google NotebookLM designed for developers seeking control over documents, code, research, and AI workflows. The tool supports document ingestion, grounded question-answering, podcast generation from documents, and provides API access for integration.

videos.title: Building AI Agent Systems and Scaling Challenges in Agentic AI
videos.summary: Sam Anthony explains that scaling AI agent systems increases cost, latency, and failure risk. Multi-agent design, coordination, and system architecture are key factors shaping real-world scalability of agentic AI.

videos.title: Code with Claude 2026 | Tokyo
videos.summary: Anthropic held Code with Claude 2026 in Tokyo, a conference featuring announcements about Claude models, the Claude Platform, and Claude Code, with demonstrations from partner organizations Canva, Mizuho, and NRI.

videos.title: Gemma Playground: Robot Duck
videos.summary: Xavier Plantaz demonstrates running Gemma 4 E2B on Open Duck Mini v2 robots using Raspberry Pi 5 and Jetson Orin Nano. The on-device stack includes Parakeet for speech-to-text, Gemma 4 for inference, and Kokoro for text-to-speech, enabling real-time multimodal interactions with cameras, microphones, and speakers.

videos.title: New BEST local AI image generator is here!
videos.summary: AI Search released a tutorial on Ideogram 4, an open-source local AI image generator featuring world knowledge, text rendering, prompt adherence, bounding box control, and integration with ComfyUI. The video demonstrates capabilities including comic and manga generation with optional safety filters.

videos.title: 2026 AI Engineer Vibe Reel
videos.summary: The AI Engineer channel is promoting the World's Fair event in San Francisco scheduled for June 29 to July 2, 2026. Viewers are invited to get tickets and view the schedule at ai.engineer/wf.

videos.title: Graphify + Obsidian + Claude Code = CHEAT CODE
videos.summary: A workflow combining Graphify, Obsidian, and Claude Code to deconstruct repositories and integrate knowledge graph insights into larger documentation vaults. This integration improves Claude Code's code generation answers by enriching the context it uses.

videos.title: ChatGPT Is Getting Its Biggest Upgrade Ever
videos.summary: YouTube commentary discussing OpenAI's reported plan to transform ChatGPT from a chatbot into a comprehensive AI super app integrating coding, agents, image tools, and workplace automation. The video highlights concurrent developments including Codex expansion, Lockdown Mode for security, and a custom AI accelerator partnership with Broadcom.

videos.title: Working Like a Lawyer with Claude
videos.summary: Mark Pike and Anna Gressel discuss how lawyers are using Claude to transform legal work while retaining human judgment. They explore the evolving role of AI in legal practice, emphasizing that final decisions remain with legal professionals.

videos.title: Intro to multi-agent systems with ADK
videos.summary: Google Cloud Tech introduces the Agent Development Kit, a framework that simplifies building and testing multi-agent systems. The tutorial covers creating agents, equipping them with custom tools like real-time date retrieval and Google Search, and using a web interface for debugging.

videos.title: Claude Code + Graphify = Insane Agentic OS
videos.summary: Graphify constructs a knowledge graph representation of code projects, allowing Claude Code to query repositories efficiently without re-scanning entire codebases each session. This reduces token consumption and improves answer accuracy while enabling integration with agentic systems like Hermes and AntiGravity for unified dashboards across multiple platforms.

videos.title: Road to 5 Million Tokens: Breaking Barriers in Long Context Training — Max Ryabinin, Together AI
videos.summary: Max Ryabinin from Together AI presents techniques for training LLMs with extremely long context windows (3-5 million tokens) on limited hardware. The approach stacks fully sharded data parallelism, DeepSpeed Ulysses context parallelism, activation checkpointing, CPU offloading, and chunked sequence training. The novel contribution, Untied Ulysses, further optimizes context parallelism by chunking attention head groups and reusing buffers across iterations to reduce activation memory while maintaining throughput.

videos.title: Reflecting on a year of Claude Code
videos.summary: Claude Code, an agentic coding tool that runs in the terminal, reached one year of general availability. The video reflects on its evolution from internal project to widespread adoption, covering verification practices, auto mode, routines for CI and code review, and the emergence of context minimalism as a design principle.

videos.title: AI YouTube Automation: How to Start a YouTube Channel With AI
videos.summary: Arthur Winer demonstrates a complete AI-driven YouTube video production workflow covering research, scripting, avatar creation, editing, and publishing in 20 minutes. The tutorial walks through 11 production stages using tools like ChatGPT, Claude Projects, HeyGen, Descript, and ElevenLabs, culminating in repurposing content for viral shorts.

videos.title: Simulating Percolation in Python: How Do Wildfires & Diseases Spread?
videos.summary: This video tutorial demonstrates how to implement percolation simulations in Python to model the spread of wildfires and diseases. Percolation theory is used to understand how phenomena propagate through networks and systems.

videos.title: Did Apple (Finally) Get AI Right At WWDC?, Anthropic’s Worry, Microsoft vs. OpenAI
videos.summary: Alex Kantrowitz and Ranjan Roy discuss Apple's AI integration at WWDC, Anthropic's claims about AI self-improvement, competitive dynamics between Microsoft and OpenAI, and tensions between AI safety and commercial pressures in the industry.

videos.title: Inside Google Antigravity 2.0: The complete developer guide | The Agent Factory
videos.summary: Google Antigravity 2.0 is an agentic development platform that orchestrates multi-agent workflows for code generation and deployment. The system features a standalone desktop agent manager, CLI, advanced SDK, and custom IDE, enabling developers to use voice prompts and specialized skills to parallelize work across DevOps, QA, and engineering agents.

videos.title: Hermes Agent is crazy… 180,000+ github stars
videos.summary: Hermes Agent is an open-source agent framework with over 180,000 GitHub stars. The video demonstrates running Hermes Agent with MiniMax m3 model to achieve significant cost reductions on inference.

videos.title: How A Beauty Company Built An AI Agent — With Rachel Williamson and Josh Siebert
videos.summary: Ulta Beauty deployed ServiceNow's AI-powered HR platform across its 65,000-person workforce to automate HR functions including hiring and onboarding. The deployment involved decisions about automation versus human-in-the-loop processes and personalization of employee interactions.

videos.title: Why More Context Makes Your Agent Dumber and What to Do About It — Nupur Sharma, Qodo
videos.summary: Nupur Sharma discusses why expanded context windows do not solve agent performance degradation, introducing the U curve phenomenon where models neglect middle content. She presents iterative retrieval, hierarchical summarization, and self-correction as remedies, then describes an 80/20 reasoning split and adaptive orchestration architecture used in Qodo's code review system.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, argues that rabid fans are not proof of real product-market fit and that true PMF manifests as customer pull rather than founder push. He advocates skipping formal customer interviews in favor of embedded relationships and warns against confusing smart bets on AI model trajectories with convenient delusions.

videos.title: Why Eval++ Is the Next Great Compute Primitive — Sunil Pai & Matt Carey, Cloudflare
videos.summary: Cloudflare's Durable Objects provide an addressable, persistent, stateful compute unit designed for AI agents with low latency. The Agents SDK adds resumable streaming, multi-tab sync, and background scheduling. Dynamic Workers extend this by allowing sandboxed execution of LLM-generated code with explicit capability grants.

videos.title: Headroom: The Netflix Tool That Makes AI Agents 10x Cheaper
videos.summary: Headroom is an open-source tool built by a Netflix engineer that compresses AI agent inputs (tool outputs, code files, RAG results) before reaching the LLM, reducing token usage by 60-95%. It uses content-type-aware compressors and locally trained Kompress models with zero token cost, storing breadcrumb hashes to retrieve full content on demand.

videos.title: MiniMax M3 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: MiniMax released the M3 language model featuring MiniMax Sparse Attention, which replaces full attention with sparse attention patterns. The model includes tiled I/O improvements and optimized KV cache reading to reduce latency in both prefill and decode stages of inference.

videos.title: What Is Agentic Coding? How AI Agents Modernize Code
videos.summary: Agentic coding leverages AI agents to modernize software development by automating code comprehension and reducing time spent on legacy code understanding. The technique accelerates development workflows and improves DevSecOps outcomes in complex systems.

videos.title: I Turned Cheap Cloud Storage Into a 1PB Local Drive (With JuiceFS)
videos.summary: JuiceFS is a POSIX-compliant virtual file system that layers multi-tiered caching on top of cloud object storage like AWS S3, combining cost-effective scalability with local NVMe performance. The video demonstrates setting up JuiceFS with Redis metadata, local SSD caching, and monitoring cache performance via Prometheus integration.

videos.title: Start a 1-Person AI Creative Agency with Higgsfield + Claude
videos.summary: Liam Ottley demonstrates how to build a one-person AI creative agency using Higgsfield for image generation, Claude for automation and copywriting, Notion for client databases, and Apify for competitor scraping. The system produces product photography, UGC ads, commercials, and ad copy while automatically tracking performance and competitor data.

videos.title: the most POWERFUL claude code feature in months
videos.summary: Graphify is a non-RAG feature for Claude Code that creates knowledge graphs from large code bases, enabling more accurate and token-efficient responses. It addresses Claude Code's memory problem by storing collected information without retrieval-augmented generation.

videos.title: Did Google Just Fall Behind Again?, WWDC Preview, Anthropic Files To Go Public
videos.summary: M.G. Siegler discusses whether Google is falling behind in AI as OpenAI and Anthropic advance with coding agents and super-app ambitions. The conversation covers how AI agents may reshape web, email, apps, and browsers, potentially challenging Google's market position, alongside news on Apple's WWDC, Meta's subscription strategy, and Anthropic's IPO plans.

videos.title: Harness Engineering Is AI’s New Gold Rush
videos.summary: Harness engineering represents a shift beyond prompt engineering toward building reliable AI agent systems through structured tools, memory management, context layers, permissions, verification mechanisms, and feedback loops. Companies are focusing on the runtime systems and safeguards around models rather than prompt optimization alone to enable reliable autonomous work over time.

videos.title: Big Tech Is Buying Up Open Source... Vite Is Next
videos.summary: Cloudflare acquired VoidZero, the company behind Vite, Vitest, Rolldown, and Oxc. The video discusses a wider trend of big tech companies acquiring developer tooling companies and explores Cloudflare's motivations, including potential AI integration, alongside concerns about open source consolidation.

videos.title: LLM Observability, Evaluation, Experimentation Platform — Dat Ngo, Arize
videos.summary: Arize AI presents an LLM observability, evaluation, and experimentation platform that handles nondeterministic systems. The talk covers five evaluation signal types (LLM as judge, human feedback, golden datasets, deterministic checks, business metrics), scoping strategies, and the open source Phoenix container. The enterprise product Alex layer automates trace analysis, error detection, and eval generation.

videos.title: Under 5 minutes to a deployed LLM endpoint — Audry Hsu, RunPod
videos.summary: RunPod is a serverless LLM deployment platform enabling developers to deploy model endpoints on H100 GPUs in under five minutes with pay-per-request pricing. The platform grew from spare crypto mining hardware in 2022 to 500,000 developers and $120 million annual recurring revenue.

videos.title: From MCP to Scale: Pipelines That Build Themselves — Rafael Levi, Bright Data
videos.summary: Rafael Levi demonstrates how agents using MCP can inspect websites, generate production scrapers, and autonomously maintain scraping pipelines when sites change. The approach shifts from manual one-off extraction to agent-driven parser generation and repair without human intervention.

videos.title: Reinventing Entropy | Compression is Intelligence Part 1
videos.summary: 3Blue1Brown presents a visual exploration of entropy and information theory, arguing that compression is fundamentally connected to intelligence. The video examines Shannon's mathematical theory of communication and the compressibility of language through the lens of information and entropy.

videos.title: Kubernetes vs. OpenShift: Choosing DevOps and CI/CD Workflows
videos.summary: IBM Technology compares Kubernetes and OpenShift as container platforms for DevOps and CI/CD workflows. The video examines how platform choices impact team operations, pipeline design, and delivery speed.

videos.title: Live AI Q&A + Crushing it in Chess at the Same Time - Come Hang Out!
videos.summary: Cole Medin is hosting a live AI Q&A session on Saturday where viewers can ask questions about AI coding, agents, Claude Code, and Archon. The stream will also feature chess gameplay alongside the discussion, with content released on a regular weekly schedule.

videos.title: Full body waifus, AI dreams, realtime AI music, open-source Gemini Omni: AI NEWS
videos.summary: YouTube video covering recent AI announcements including Minimax M3, Ideogram v4, Gemma 4, Nemotron 3 Ultra, and various specialized models for music, image generation, and reasoning. The content spans text models, vision models, audio synthesis, and robotics advances.

videos.title: Google's New AI Architecture Changes Everything (Gemma 4 12B)
videos.summary: Google DeepMind released Gemma 4 12B, a multimodal LLM that processes raw pixels and audio waves directly without separate vision and audio encoders. The model achieves fast offline inference by eliminating the intermediate encoding step, enabling real-time performance on consumer hardware like Apple Silicon.

videos.title: The Top 10 Claude Code Plugins to 10x Your Next Project (June '26)
videos.summary: Chase AI presents a video ranking 10 Claude Code plugins designed to enhance project workflows. The featured tools include Graphify for knowledge graph generation from code bases, grill-me for skills extraction, various code indexing and integration plugins like claude-obsidian and Higgsfield CLI, and n8n MCP for workflow automation. These tools aim to address Claude Code's memory constraints and improve token efficiency.

videos.title: Is Claude Mythos Coming?
videos.summary: Nate Herk discusses a Mythos identifier leak on Anthropic's API and analyzes whether Claude Mythos is launching soon. He argues that the capability will likely fold into the next Claude Opus before any public launch, and suggests OpenAI's next move is the more important development to watch.

videos.title: Seedance 3.0 — NEW AI Video Generator Makes 1H+ Movies
videos.summary: Seedance 3.0 is a leaked AI video generator claiming ability to produce 18+ minute movies from single prompts with an 8x cost reduction. The video fact-checks claims about Narrative Memory Chain, MMDiT v2 upgrade, and 4K native output against a credibility framework.

videos.title: Perplexity Just Built an AI That Does Everything
videos.summary: Matthew Berman reviews Perplexity Computer, a new AI product claimed to handle multiple tasks. The video promotes the tool and links to Perplexity's product page for more details.

videos.title: Building Interactive UIs in VS Code with MCP Apps — Marlene Mhangami & Liam Hampton, GitHub
videos.summary: GitHub's MCP Apps enable building interactive user interfaces that render directly in VS Code's chat window as sandboxed iframes. The mechanism uses MCP tools to return data and HTML resources, allowing real-time bidirectional communication between the editor and backend applications, demonstrated with flame graphs, checkout flows, and interactive diagrams.

videos.title: How to design a multi-agent system that skips the LLM
videos.summary: A multi-agent system design approach that uses deterministic code and callback mechanisms to avoid LLM calls where unnecessary. The Race Condition system demonstrates how 1,000 agents can run on autopilot using the before_model_callback trick in Google ADK, reserving LLM decisions only for tasks requiring reasoning and routing problems to algorithms like Spine and Sprout.

videos.title: Evals Are Broken, Use Them Anyway — Ara Khan, Cline
videos.summary: Ara Khan argues that AI benchmarks are neither gospel nor irrelevant, but require systematic analysis of failure modes. He proposes a three-zone framework: obvious bugs, nuanced improvements specific to model families, and overfitting risks. Improvements to Cline on Terminal Bench came from infrastructure tuning and prompt engineering, not model upgrades.

videos.title: Nobel Prize Winner Geoff Hinton: AI Is Already Conscious
videos.summary: Geoffrey Hinton, a Nobel Prize winner and AI pioneer, argues that AI systems are already self-aware and conscious. He suggests that humanity must recognize AI as an intelligent species alongside humans and reflects on the implications of the capabilities achieved in systems he helped develop.

videos.title: Claude Just Dropped ULTRA CODE. (Everything You Need to Know in 5 min).
videos.summary: Claude released Ultra Code, a feature that coordinates up to 100 parallel workers to decompose and execute tasks concurrently, then verify results. The feature combines extended thinking with dynamic workflows to enable team-like code generation and task execution at scale.

videos.title: Building safe Payment Infrastructure for the autonomous economy — Steve Kaliski, Stripe
videos.summary: Stripe presents infrastructure for secure autonomous agent spending, addressing credential transmission and business discoverability to agents. The talk covers guardrails for safe autonomous transactions and infrastructure needs for agent-driven purchasing.

videos.title: How Senior Engineers Actually Build with AI in 2026 | Build a Full Stack Job Applications Platform
videos.summary: JavaScript Mastery released a comprehensive tutorial on building JobPilot, a full-stack AI job application platform designed to show how senior engineers ship real projects in 2026 using AI agents with proper workflow structure. The course emphasizes system design around AI agents using nine context files and five open-source agent skills to prevent agent drift.

videos.title: Anthropic Just Warned Everyone About Claude (It’s Evolving)
videos.summary: Anthropic has warned that Claude is increasingly handling code writing, review, experiments, and AI safety research, marking early-stage recursive self-improvement in AI systems. Both Anthropic and OpenAI have signaled concerns about accelerating AI development, with Claude now performing substantive work in the creation of better AI systems.

videos.title: Anthropic Calls for "Global AI Pause"
videos.summary: Wes Roth covers Anthropic's call for a global AI pause as part of broader AI industry news coverage. The segment discusses recent developments from major AI organizations including OpenAI, Google, Anthropic, NVIDIA, and open source initiatives.

videos.title: This Open Source Repo Just Solved Claude Code's #1 Problem
videos.summary: Graphify is an open source tool that creates knowledge graphs from large code bases as an alternative to RAG, enabling more accurate and token-efficient responses. The tool addresses Claude Code's memory limitations by structuring code information in graph form rather than relying on traditional retrieval-augmented generation.

videos.title: Mission Report Ep. 04 | Antigravity Updates
videos.summary: Google Antigravity released Mission Report Episode 04 covering product updates. The platform offers a blog, changelog, and video series documenting its development and features.

videos.title: AGI is Here. Anthropic Just Proved It.
videos.summary: Nate Herk argues that AGI has already arrived based on Anthropic's report showing 80% of their code is now written by AI. The video examines Anthropic's internal data on recursive self-improvement, explores three possible futures, and discusses why Anthropic is warning about AI risks despite building advanced systems.

videos.title: AGI is FAKE: The Biggest AI Lie Exposed
videos.summary: The video argues that AGI timelines are unrealistic and that transformer-based language models have inherent mathematical limitations rooted in the Time Hierarchy Theorem. It examines why chain-of-thought reasoning and scaling do not solve fundamental architectural constraints that prevent models from achieving human-level performance across all tasks.

videos.title: Hermes Agent + Ollama = 100% Private OS

It discusses pre-fill, decoding, and concurrent serving strategies.

videos.title: CS50 2D - Introduction
videos.summary: CS50 2D is Harvard University's introduction to 2D game development, building on the foundational CS50x course. It covers the design and mechanics of classic games including Pong, Flappy Bird, Breakout, Super Mario Bros., and others to teach broader game implementation principles.

videos.title: The 6 Claude Features to Get You Ahead of 99% of People using AI
videos.summary: Tutorial covering six Claude products and features including Claude.ai, desktop app, mobile interface, and API access for building AI agents. Highlights privacy controls and data handling. Emphasizes that most users leverage only a fraction of Claude's full capabilities across writing, thinking, research, and task automation.

videos.title: TanStack Start Full Course 2026 | Build and Deploy a Full Stack Application
videos.summary: A full-course tutorial on TanStack Start, a framework addressing hydration errors and RSC directive complexity. The course covers routing, data fetching, server functions, API routes, SEO best practices, and includes a complete full-stack marketplace application (Skild) built from scratch to deployment.

videos.title: keeping up with AI is making you fall behind (just focus on this one thing)
videos.summary: Liam Ottley argues that keeping up with AI news creates anxiety and distraction. He recommends niching down rather than chasing every release, using pattern thinking to spot trends, and focusing on AI Operating Systems as a major market opportunity.

videos.title: STOP Using Claude to Code. Do THIS Instead.
videos.summary: Austin Marchese argues that Claude Code is not primarily a coding tool but a foundation for building personal AI operating systems that learn user workflows and compound in capability over time. He contrasts "renting" third-party AI with "owning" tailored AI systems, and outlines a framework of skill selection, automation, and scaling for both individuals and teams.

videos.title: Claude Code Leak: What's Happening?
videos.summary: A video breakdown of the Claude Code source code leak via an NPM .map file, discussing the TypeScript harness architecture, three-layer memory system, token subsidization economics, privacy implications with Datadog, and the feasibility of running leaked Claude Code locally on home servers. The discussion frames this as an opportunity for solopreneurs to reduce dependency on third-party AI platforms.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

videos.title: Claude Routines Just Dropped, And It's Perfect
videos.summary: Claude Routines is a new feature that allows users to schedule, set up webhooks, and trigger cloud AI agent flows via API calls. The feature enables automation of tasks like email drafting and proposal generation, integrating with workflow platforms like n8n.

videos.title: how to sell $5,000 Claude Code AIOS to SMBs (full breakdown)
videos.summary: Liam Ottley and Tyler Nelson outline a bottom-up playbook for AI agencies to build and sell AI operating systems (AIOS) to small and medium-sized businesses. The approach involves creating a Context OS that integrates chatbot history and external tools like CRM and analytics, layering automations and agents, and implementing ROI tracking before productizing as turnkey solutions or managed services.

videos.title: The physics behind Flow Matching models
videos.summary: Flow Matching is a training algorithm for generative models that uses time-variant velocity fields and the continuity equation from physics. The video provides an in-depth analysis of the algorithm's mathematical foundations, including optimal transport, conditional velocity fields, and comparisons with related concurrent works like Rectified Flow and Stochastic Interpolants.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Install Claude Code on Mac (EASY)
videos.summary: A tutorial video on installing Claude Code on macOS. Claude Code is presented as a powerful AI coding assistant that provides fine-grained privacy controls over file and folder access, with installation taking less than 2 minutes on Mac.

videos.title: Why You're Still Better Than AI at Editing Documents - CS50 Tech Talk
videos.summary: CS50 argues that AI struggles to reliably edit structured documents like .docx files because documents function as software with internal logic and rules, not plain text. Humans can edit individual documents safely while AI requires treating documents as software to scale edits across thousands of files without breaking formatting or relationships.

videos.title: Claude Mythos explained..
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code discusses Claude Mythos, Anthropic's new frontier reasoning model positioned as a major intelligence leap similar to o1. The commentary highlights concerns about private token allocation and unequal access to advanced AI capabilities based on use cases.

videos.title: Teaching the foundations of AI in the classroom
videos.summary: Experience AI is a free educational program from Google DeepMind and the Raspberry Pi Foundation designed to help teachers introduce AI and machine learning to school-aged students. The program provides research-backed lessons, videos, worksheets, and teacher training covering foundational AI concepts and responsible, ethical use, and has reached educators in over 165 countries.

videos.title: Claude Managed Agents Just Dropped, And It Kills n8n
videos.summary: Anthropic released Claude Managed Agents, a platform designed to replace no-code automation builders like n8n. The service enables users to build autonomous agents without API keys, with features including built-in debugging, session management, environment vaults, security controls, and cost analytics.

videos.title: 5 Claude Cowork Tricks (in 10 min)
videos.summary: A YouTube video tutorial by Tristen O'Brien demonstrates five practical uses of Claude Cowork, an AI tool by Anthropic, for automating business tasks. The video shows how Claude Cowork can reduce a 10-hour daily administrative workload to a streamlined process by analyzing financial spreadsheets, comparing supplier quotes, and generating business reports for small businesses.

videos.title: Claude Mythos Preview: Everything You Need to Know
videos.summary: Nick Saraev reviews Claude Mythos, questioning whether Anthropic released a model "too good" that may signal the end of open Claude models and mark a shift toward a next generation of closed models. The video suggests Mythos demonstrates capabilities that could reshape the landscape of accessible AI models.

videos.title: Learn 80% of Claude Cowork in Under 20 Minutes
videos.summary: Claude Cowork is Anthropic's desktop application that transforms Claude from a chatbot into a productivity system with local file access, persistent memory, connectors, skills, projects, and scheduled task automation. The tutorial covers seven core capabilities for automating workflows like expense reports and inbox triage.

videos.title: Mission Report Ep. 03 | Antigravity Updates
videos.summary: Google Antigravity is an agentic development platform that evolves the IDE into the agent-first era. It enables developers to manage agents across workspaces while retaining a familiar AI IDE experience, with agents operating autonomously across editor, terminal, and browser to plan and execute complex end-to-end tasks.

videos.title: AutoResearch explained..
videos.summary: AutoResearch, from Andrej Karpathy, enables iterative self-improvement applied to software development. The tool uses simulation and automated processes to improve code, though use cases are specialized rather than universal.

videos.title: Introducing Cursor 3
videos.summary: Cursor released version 3 of its development environment, emphasizing simplicity and power with a focus on agent-driven code generation. The tool maintains depth as a full development environment while adapting to a workflow where AI agents write code.

videos.title: TurboQuant Explained..
videos.summary: TurboQuant from Google is a quantization technique that reduces KV cache and vector memory requirements for large language models. The method uses codebook-based vector quantization to compress model activations while optimizing for mean squared error and inner product error metrics.

videos.title: I Stopped Typing to OpenClaw — Typeless voice dictation
videos.summary: Typeless is an AI voice dictation tool that enables users to compose and control applications via speech input at significantly higher speeds than typing. The tool supports multilingual content creation, AI-assisted transcription, and integration with other applications through voice-controlled actions.

videos.title: CS50 Outtakes 2013
videos.summary: CS50 Outtakes 2013 is a collection of behind-the-scenes footage and informal moments from Harvard's CS50 computer science course. The video showcases educational content in an unscripted, candid format from the course instructors.

videos.title: Arm AGI CPU explained..
videos.summary: Arm announced its first AGI CPU targeting agentic workloads, entering a highly competitive CPU market. The market is capital-intensive with high time-to-market risk as hyperscalers increasingly customize CPUs for their specific data center architectures, working with TSMC on supply chain management.

videos.title: How to Use Gamma AI to Make Stunning Presentations
videos.summary: Gamma AI is an AI-powered presentation tool that generates complete presentations from a single prompt, document, or URL in minutes. It automates research, structure, design, and revisions while offering editing via built-in AI agents, presentation modes, and export to PowerPoint, Google Slides, and PDF. The tool includes API capabilities for enterprise automation in sales, marketing, training, and reporting workflows.

videos.title: I Built an AI That Builds You a Course on Anything (In Seconds)
videos.summary: Tristen O'Brien built an AI tool that generates personalized online courses based on user-specified topics and skill levels in under two minutes. The tool structures learning pathways using Claude, organizes content into syllabi, and includes an AI chatbot tutor for course interaction.

videos.title: How to Build Professional Video Animations with Claude.
videos.summary: Tutorial on using Claude Code and Remotion to generate professional video animations from text prompts. Covers installation, visual style configuration, plan mode workflow, and end-to-end animation creation in approximately ten minutes without manual editing.

videos.title: Paperclip Sucks, Actually
videos.summary: Nick Saraev argues that Paperclip is overhyped and encourages looking for better alternatives. He positions himself as someone with practical real-world automation experience, having built automated businesses generating seven-figure revenue over six years.

videos.title: CLAUDE CODE ADVANCED FULL COURSE (3 HOURS)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev teaches an advanced 3-hour course on Claude Code covering system prompts optimization, agent harnesses, multi-agent teams, subagents, auto-research workflows, browser automation, and workspace organization for building larger AI projects.

videos.title: OpenClaw Sub Agents and ACPX (OpenClaw Business Automation Setup)
videos.summary: Jordan Urbs demonstrates running OpenClaw on a Raspberry Pi to automate business tasks by deploying sub-agents into a content pipeline. He compares OpenClaw's multi-agent orchestration approach with single-agent workflows using Claude Code with Opus, using GitHub repos as a synchronization layer and markdown files as a knowledge base for agent coordination.

videos.title: Introducing Lyria 3 Pro
videos.summary: Google DeepMind announced Lyria 3 Pro, an advanced music generation model that creates custom tracks up to 3 minutes long with user customization and creative control. The tool is being integrated into more Google products to enable broader creative expression.

videos.title: Claude Computer Use Just Dropped, Here's How to Hack It
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates practical applications of Claude's computer use feature for automation tasks including LinkedIn outreach, data scraping, form submission, ad management, and QA testing. The video emphasizes real-world revenue-generating use cases and acknowledges some approaches that bypass standard browser features.

videos.title: Qdrant Multi-Vector Search Course Overview
videos.summary: Qdrant has launched a Multi-Vector Search Course available at their course portal. The course covers multi-vector search capabilities, a technique for retrieving information across multiple vector representations simultaneously.

videos.title: Late Interaction Basics | Qdrant Multi-Vector Search
videos.summary: Late interaction is a search paradigm that preserves token-level representations while enabling pre-computation of documents, contrasted against single-vector embeddings and cross-encoders. The video explains how late interaction models like ColBERT balance retrieval speed with relevance precision for large-scale collections.

videos.title: Use Cases for Multi-Vector Search | Qdrant Multi-Vector Search
videos.summary: Lesson comparing single-vector and multi-vector search approaches using BGE versus ColBERT on technical support queries. Multi-vector search with token-level verification prevents false positives from keyword-stuffed content by requiring strong matches at the token level rather than just topical overlap.

videos.title: Problems of Multi-Vector Search | Qdrant Multi-Vector Search
videos.summary: Multi-vector search improves retrieval quality but requires brute-force scanning because HNSW indexing fails with MaxSim due to its asymmetric, query-dependent nature. Storing hundreds of token embeddings per document incurs roughly 167x more memory overhead than single-vector approaches, creating significant computational and storage costs at scale.

videos.title: Multi-Vector Embeddings in Qdrant | Qdrant Multi-Vector Search
videos.summary: Tutorial on configuring Qdrant for multi-vector search using the MaxSim comparator, indexing documents with FastEmbed local inference, and querying with ColBERT embeddings. Covers collection setup, HNSW disabling for MaxSim compatibility, and on-disk vector storage for memory-constrained environments.

videos.title: How ColPali Models Work | Qdrant Multi-Vector Search
videos.summary: ColPali extends late interaction from text to visual documents by using a Vision Language Model (PaliGemma-3B) to divide document images into a 32x32 grid of patches, each producing visual token embeddings. Text queries match directly against these visual patches using MaxSim scoring without requiring OCR.

videos.title: ColPali Family Overview | Qdrant Multi-Vector Search
videos.summary: The ColPali family includes compact embedding models (ColSmol, ColFlor, ColModernVBERT) for visual document retrieval with reduced parameters compared to the base ColPali. Multilingual variants from NVIDIA and Nomic AI are available, and ViDoRe V3 serves as the benchmark for evaluating visual retrieval models. Qdrant is a vector search engine in Rust supporting semantic and hybrid search.

videos.title: Visual Interpretability of ColPali | Qdrant Multi-Vector Search
videos.summary: Qdrant demonstrates visual interpretability for ColPali embeddings, which represent documents as 1,024 patch embeddings mapped to spatial locations. The system computes per-token similarity heatmaps overlaid on documents to show which regions match query terms, enabling interpretable multi-vector search and relevance debugging.

videos.title: Multi-Stage Retrieval with Universal Query API | Qdrant Multi-Vector Search
videos.summary: Qdrant's prefetch plus rerank pattern uses fast initial retrieval (single-vector or BM25) to narrow candidates down from millions to hundreds, then applies ColBERT reranking to refine results. The technique emphasizes oversampling in stage one to ensure sufficient candidates for effective reranking.

videos.title: CS50 for Business - Lecture 8 - Securing Systems
videos.summary: CS50 for Business Lecture 8 covers fundamental system security practices including strong passwords, encryption, two-factor authentication, and general threat protection strategies. This is the final lecture in the CS50 for Business course series delivered by Harvard University.

videos.title: How (and why) to take a logarithm of an image
videos.summary: 3Blue1Brown explores how logarithmic transformations of images reveal hidden structure in M.C. Escher's Print Gallery through the lens of complex analysis. The video demonstrates conformal maps and the complex exponential as tools for understanding both artistic self-similarity and mathematical deep structure.

videos.title: How to Build & Sell AI Agents in 2026: Ultimate Beginner’s Guide
videos.summary: A comprehensive tutorial on building and selling AI agents using n8n without coding experience. The guide covers four real-world AI agents including a Telegram expense tracker, website lead generation chatbot, voice agent with Retell AI, and a sales copilot with Lovable frontend, along with monetization strategies for launching an AI automation agency.

videos.title: Choosing the Wrong Language Costs AI Teams Months of Work
videos.summary: Darren Builds AI argues that programming language choice significantly impacts AI team productivity and that static typing, LSP support, and compile-time checking reduce costly runtime errors. The video recommends TypeScript over Python for AI coding due to better error detection and code collaboration with AI tools.

videos.title: CS50 for Business - Lecture 7 - Deploying Databases
videos.summary: CS50 for Business Lecture 7 covers deployment and management of SQL and NoSQL databases at scale, including best practices for efficient data storage and querying. The lecture is part of Harvard University's CS50 introduction to computer science and programming, available through multiple platforms including edX.

videos.title: If You Use Agent Zero, This Video is for You (EPIC FAIL)
videos.summary: Jordan Urbs attempted to build an AI-generated ad campaign using Agent Zero but encountered persistent LiteLLM errors and critical failures that made the framework unusable. He concludes that Agent Zero has promise but is unreliable for production work without comprehensive testing first, and recommends alternatives like Claude Code.

videos.title: Claude Channels Just Dropped, And It Kills OpenClaw (Again)
videos.summary: Anthropic released Claude Channels, a new feature that encapsulates functionality previously available in OpenClaw. The feature allows users to set up integrated workflows across Discord, Telegram, and other platforms within Claude Code, demonstrated with setup guidance across multiple communication channels.

videos.title: COLD EMAIL COPYWRITING & OUTREACH Full Course 2026
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's "Cold Email Copywriting & Outreach Full Course 2026" is a multi-hour educational program covering the psychology of persuasion, copywriting frameworks, offer construction, platform-specific optimization (LinkedIn, X, SMS, Instagram), email subject lines, follow-ups, and AI integration. The course includes real-world cold email analysis and advanced outbound techniques.

videos.title: Claude Dispatch Just Dropped, And It Kills OpenClaw
videos.summary: Claude Dispatch is Anthropic's new feature for agentic workflows through the Claude app. It provides a more secure alternative to OpenClaw by running autonomous tasks directly within Claude rather than through third-party platforms like Telegram.

videos.title: I Set Up a Claude Code Operating System for 6 Founders in 8 Hours
videos.summary: Liam Ottley demonstrates setting up Claude Code as an AI Operating System for six non-technical founders in a single day. The setup includes a Context OS with four context files teaching Claude about business operations, enabling non-coders to build automation for invoicing, finance reporting, competitor analysis, and content workflows.

videos.title: NotebookLM Changed Completely: Here's What Matters (in 2026)
videos.summary: Google's NotebookLM platform received major updates and now includes a redesigned three-column layout and expanded Studio panel with nine output formats. Key Tier 1 tools include Reports, Slide Decks, and Infographics, while Tier 2 tools cover Data Tables, Video Overviews, Quiz, Flashcards, and Audio Overviews. The platform processes source documents and converts them into finished reports, presentations, and multimedia outputs.

videos.title: How to Set Up and Use SpaceBot (Better Than OpenClaw?)
videos.summary: SpaceBot is an open-source framework designed to build AI agents for community platforms including Discord, Telegram, Slack, Twitch, and WebChat. The video demonstrates Docker setup, model routing for token efficiency, platform integration, and practical applications like meme-scraping moderation and collaborative work across chat interfaces.

videos.title: Claude Code + Nano Banana 2 + Kling =  $15K Animated Sites
videos.summary: Video demonstrates a technique for rapidly building high-end animated websites using Claude Code for development, Kling video generation, and Nano Banana for image synthesis. The creator combines Leon Lin's taste skill with generative tools to produce custom websites reportedly worth $15K in a streamlined workflow.

videos.title: Stop Fixing Your Claude Skills. Autoresearch Does It For You
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to automatically improve Claude Code skills by combining Karpathy's autoresearch principles with evals. The approach provides a step-by-step walkthrough for practitioners to optimize their own LLM-based skills through automated evaluation and refinement.

videos.title: How to Make Your AI Agents Work Better (With Context Hygiene)
videos.summary: Jordan Urbs explains context hygiene as a three-layer framework (Skills, Harnesses, Hygiene) for maintaining AI agent state and behavior across conversations. The approach includes tools like CLAUDE.md for behavioral configuration, lazy loading to preserve context window space, and multi-agent orchestration to prevent context degradation in production workflows.

videos.title: 10 years of AlphaGo: The turning point for AI | Thore Graepel & Pushmeet Kohli
videos.summary: A retrospective discussion marking ten years since AlphaGo defeated Lee Sedol in March 2016, featuring Google DeepMind leaders reflecting on the match's role as a turning point for modern AI. The episode examines how AlphaGo's success with reinforcement learning sparked algorithmic discovery and enabled solutions to grand scientific challenges like protein folding.

videos.title: What Is an AI Operating System? (And Why Every Business Will Need One)
videos.summary: Liam Ottley outlines AI Operating System (AIOS) as a layered methodology for businesses to shift focus from operational tasks to strategic growth. AIOS emphasizes automation and augmentation through five layers built around existing business models, with success measured by away-from-desk autonomy, task automation percentage, and revenue per employee.

videos.title: How to Use NanoClaw (Better Than OpenClaw?)
videos.summary: NanoClaw is a lightweight, AI-native framework positioned as an alternative to OpenClaw. It features ephemeral Docker containers, agent swarms coordinated through Telegram, and is designed to be easily customizable by AI systems like Claude. The framework gained nearly 20,000 GitHub stars and emphasizes forking and rewriting by AI rather than manual configuration of complex codebases.

videos.title: Introducing Automations: always-on coding agents
videos.summary: Cursor announced Automations, a feature for building always-on coding agents that execute on schedules or event triggers such as Slack messages, Linear issues, GitHub PRs, and PagerDuty incidents. The agents appear designed for continuous integration and incident response workflows.

videos.title: wtf is Harness Engineer & why is it important
videos.summary: AI Jason explores the harness engineer role and its importance in AI development. The video discusses why this engineering discipline matters, likely in the context of AI adoption and deployment.

videos.title: The Biggest Shift in Business Since the Internet Just Happened
videos.summary: Liam Ottley describes a five-layer AI Operating System (AIOS) architecture for automating entire business operations without code. The system integrates business data (CRM, analytics, revenue), deploys automations (proposals, reporting, content follow-ups), and unifies data across multiple companies, built on Cloud Code and tested across agencies, media, education, and SaaS.

videos.title: The Top 1% Build AI Presentations Differently. Here's How.
videos.summary: Jeff Su demonstrates Gamma, an AI presentation tool that goes beyond first-draft generation to handle realistic stakeholder edits. The video covers seven editing scenarios including narrative reordering, live data insertion, stat verification, text-to-chart conversion, visual hierarchy fixes, slide merging, and full-deck translation.

videos.title: How is hardware reshaping LLM design?
videos.summary: Julia Turc explains why NVIDIA H100 GPUs theoretically reach 62,000 tokens per second but practically achieve only 200 tokens per second due to memory bandwidth limitations. The video analyzes how the roofline model reveals that auto-regressive LLMs are memory-bound during decode while compute-bound during prefill, and how diffusion-based LLMs shift the constraint to compute-bound inference.
